title: gwnbr: Geographically Weighted Negative Binomial Regression in Python
description: The first open-source Python implementation of Geographically Weighted Negative Binomial Regression (GWNBR), translated from the SAS macro of Silva and Rodrigues (2014). Includes three model classes (GWNBR, GWNBRg, GWPR) with adaptive k-NN bisquare and Gaussian kernel functions, Golden Section Search bandwidth selection via AICc and cross-validation, local coefficient surfaces, significance testing with multiple-testing correction, and visualisation utilities. Applied to Maryland census tract crash-socioeconomic data.
references: Silva, A. R. and Rodrigues, T. C. V. (2014). Geographically Weighted Negative Binomial Regression - Incorporating Overdispersion. Statistics and Computing, 24, 769-783. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11222-013-9445-4
Nakaya, T., Fotheringham, A. S., Brunsdon, C. and Charlton, M. (2005). Geographically Weighted Poisson Regression for Disease Association Mapping. Statistics in Medicine, 24, 2695-2717.
Fotheringham, A. S., Brunsdon, C. and Charlton, M. (2002). Geographically Weighted Regression. Wiley.
